message:
feat(security,share,startup): symlink escape prevention across all I/O surfaces, startup database self-heal, share wizard improvements

Symlink security hardening (~60% of diff):
New path_existing_prefix_has_symlink() function walks every component of
a path checking for symlinks via symlink_metadata, applied as
defense-in-depth across nearly every I/O surface:
- Storage layer: FileLock acquire/cleanup/metadata, git index lock ops,
  attachment storage (cached and raw), archive repo/project root
  validation now uses canonical_repo_root
- Server: snapshot temp dir selection, static file web root serving,
  retention cycle scanning, archive browser TUI, project listing/metadata
- TUI persistence: all file reads/writes for macros, presets, palette
  usage, dismissed hints, and preference export/import reject symlinked
  paths; file writes switched to atomic write-via-rename pattern
Every hardening point has #[cfg(unix)] test coverage creating symlinks
and verifying rejection.

Startup database self-heal:
Both HTTP and stdio server startup paths now call
run_startup_database_self_heal() after clearing DB blockers. Diagnoses
database via doctor_database_fix_strategy and dispatches to repair or
full archive reconstruction. Recovery operations gated through
RecoveryAdmissionController with exponential backoff and thread-local
re-entrancy depth guard to prevent recursive recovery. Stdio path
upgraded from warning-on-probe-failure to hard-fail.

Share wizard improvements:
- Default GitHub Pages branch changed from gh-pages to main throughout
- Unknown provider returns structured JSON error (PROVIDER_UNKNOWN)
- GitHub repo identifier validation (normalize_github_repo_identifier)
- GitHub Pages plan resolves git repo root, scopes git add/commit/push
  to specific paths, validates output dir is inside repo, correctly
  computes URLs for user pages repos (user.github.io)
- resolve_deploy_tooling_repo_root no longer falls back to unrelated CWD
- All providers honor explicit base_url over auto-generated URLs

Minor fixes:
- search_v3_bench requires tantivy-engine feature
- output::info() helper for blue informational messages
- report_success clears failed-attempt window instead of appending

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -100,6 +100,7 @@ harness = false
 [[bench]]
 name = "search_v3_bench"
 harness = false
+required-features = ["tantivy-engine"]
 
 [[bench]]
 name = "two_tier_bench"
